original: Origin of the Saka Era in India
St. Simplicius is elected to succeed Pope Hilarius
Glycerius appointed a puppet Emperor of the Western Empire by Burgundian king and patrician Gundobad (deposed 474)
Ostrogoten King Theodorik the Great beats Odoaker
Statute of Rhuddlan incorporates the Principality of Wales into England
Austrian civil war ends
Bishop Gabriele Condulmer elected as Pope Eugene IV
Ferrante (later Ferdinand I), illegitimate son of Alfonso the Magnanimous, King of Naples, made Duke of Calabria and heir to the kingdom
Battle of Toro, during the War of the Castilian Succession, is inconclusive; Isabella of Castile claims a win for her and Ferdinand of Aragon against the Portuguese, ending the threat of invasion
The Olympic Theatre, designed by Andrea Palladio, is inaugurated in Vicenza.
Trinity College Dublin and its library is founded by royal charter by Elizabeth I on the former site of the Priory of All Hallows
1st tavern in Boston (Mass) opens (Samuel Cole)
Duke Bernard of Saxe-Weimar occupies Rheinfelden
First Battle of Tobago: Dutch fleet under Jacob Binckes forces a French squadron led by Jean II d'Estrées to retreat off Tobago in the Caribbean Sea during the Franco-Dutch War
American commodore Esek Hopkins occupies Nassau, Bahamas
First whale harpooned in the Pacific Ocean by English ship Emilia off the coast of South America
1st US internal revenue act (taxing distilled spirits & carriages)
Congress establishes US Mint
Richard Allen founded African Methodist Episcopal Church
1st US Jewish governor, David Emanuel, takes office in Georgia
1st impeachment trial of a US federal judge, John Pickering, begins
Colégio Militar is founded in Portugal by Colonel Teixeira Rebello
Louisiana-Missouri Territory forms
Office of Surgeon General of the US Army forms
US declares war on Algiers for taking US prisoners & demanding tribute
Mississippi Territory is divided into Alabama Territory & Mississippi
Missouri Compromise passes, allowing Missouri to join the United States despite slavery still being legal there.
American tailor and inventor Thomas L. Jennings (30) receives US patent for his "dry-scouring" process. the forerunner of today’s modern dry-cleaning
Congress authorizes a US mint at New Orleans, Louisiana; coins identified with mint mark 'O'
Congress increases US Supreme Court membership from 7 to 9
Rebellion at Pelee Island, Ontario, Canada
1st US child labor law regulating working hours passed in Massachusetts
Congress appropriates $30,000 "to test the practicability of establishing a system of electro-magnetic telegraphs" by the US
Florida becomes 27th state of the Union
For the first time the US Senate overrides presidential (Tyler) veto
US Congress authorizes ocean mail contracts for foreign mail delivery
US Post Office Department is authorized to issue postage stamps
Gold Coinage Act authorizes $20 Double Eagle gold coin and the $1 Gold Liberty coin in the USA
Territory of Minnesota organizes
US Home Department (later renamed the Department of the Interior) established by Congress
Congress authorizes smallest US silver coin (3 cent piece)
Transcontinental railroad survey is authorized by US Congress
US Assay Office in NYC authorized
US Congress approves $30,000 to test camels for military use
US Congress authorizes registered mail
Second Opium War: France and the United Kingdom declare war on China
Union forces under General Pope lay siege to New Madrid, Missouri (US Civil War)
1st US wartime military conscription bill enacted
Congress authorizes a US mint at Carson City, Nevada; coins identified with mint mark 'CC'
Federal ironclad ships bombard Fort McAllister, Georgia
Free city delivery replaces zone postage; 449 letter carriers hired
Gold certificates (currency) authorized by US Congress
Idaho Territory incorporated from parts of area split from Dakota, Nebraska, and Washington territories; now consists of states of Idaho. Montana, and most of Wyoming
Opening of the Hongkong and Shanghai Banking Corporation, the founding member of the HSBC Group.
US Bureau of Refugees, Freedmen, & Abandoned Lands established byAbraham Lincolnto help destitute free blacks
University of South Carolina opens to all races
US Congress changes Indian tribes status from independent to dependent
US Congress establishes the civil service system
US Congress & government retroactively raise own salaries
US Congress authorizes federal departmental postage stamps
US Congress enacts the Comstock Law, making it illegal to send any "obscene, lewd, or lascivious" books through the mail
First recognized hockey game is played at the Victoria Skating Rink in Montreal, according to the International Ice Hockey Federation, though it likely occurred much earlier
US Congress authorizes 20 cent coin, lasts only 3 years
Treaty of San Stefano signed by Russian and Ottoman empires grants independence to Bulgaria after 500 years of Turkish rule
1st female lawyer heard by US Supreme Court (Belva Ann Bennett Lockwood)
US Geological Survey director authorized in Department of the Interior
NY Steam Corp begins distributing steam to Manhattan buildings
Congress authorizes the 1st steel vessels in US navy
1st US state (California) establishes a permanent forest commission
American Telephone & Telegraph (AT&T) incorporates
US Congress passes Indian Appropriations Act (Indians wards of federal government)
US Post Office offers special delivery for 1st-class mail
American Protective Association forms (anti-Catholic) in Clinton Iowa
US President Harrison announced the government would open the 1.9 million-acre tract of Indian Territory for settlement precisely at noon on April 22
The Penalty Spot Kick rule in Association Football is conceived, but does not come into effect until the next season.
US Congress creates Courts of Appeal
US Congress creates Office of Superintendent of Immigration (Treasury Dept)
US Congress establishes the Office of Immigration
1st cattle tuberculosis test in US made, Villa Nova, Penn
US Congress authorizes 1st federal road agency, in Department of Agriculture
US Mint issues Queen Isabella commemorative silver quarter in conjunction with the World’s Columbian Exposition
"NY Atlantis" 1st Greek-language publication in US begins
4th & last British government of Gladstone resigns
US Congress authorizes Lafayette silver dollar
US Congress creates National Bureau of Standards, in Department of Commerce
North Carolina becomes 1st state requiring registration of nurses
US Forest Service forms
Vuia I aircraft built by Romanian Traian Vuia tested in France
1st US federal cemetery with Union & Confederate graves opens, Missouri
US National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics (NACA) created, the predecessor of NASA
First major strike of the Russian "February Revolution" starts at the giant Putilov factory in Petrograd [OS=Feb 18]
German Foreign Secretary Arthur Zimmermann publicly admits the "Zimmermann Telegram" is genuine; revelation generates support for the US declaration of war on Germany in April.
Mexico and the USA renew diplomatic relations
US Congress passes 1st excess profits tax on corporations
Facing internal counterrevolutionary pressures and external German offensive, Bolsheviks forced to sign harsh Treaty of Brest-Litovsk with Germany and Austria
Richard Goering's "Seeschlacht" premieres in Berlin
1st international air mail service from US, Seattle-Victoria, BC
Communist Party in Germany announces a general strike
Montreal Canadiens scores NHL record 16 goals beating Quebec Bulldogs

The Asiatic Inquiry Commission, established by the South African Government, proposes a system of voluntary repatriation and segregation of Indians and prohibits Indians from buying agricultural land in a specified area along the coast
Italian fascists occupy Fiume & Rijeka
WWJ-AM in Detroit MI begins radio transmissions
Time magazine publishes 1st issue featuring Joseph G. Cannon (Speaker of US House of Representatives)
US Senate rejects membership of the International Court of Justice, The Hague
German & Turkish friendship/trade treaty signed
Sean O'Casey's "Juno & the Paycock" premieres in Dublin
International Greyhound Racing Association formed (Miami, Fla)

"The Star-Spangled Banner" officially becomes the US national anthem by congressional resolution; lyrics by Francis Scott Key in 1814 are set to John Stafford Smith's 18th-century tune "The Anacreontic Song"
German presidential candidate Earnest Thalmann (KPD) arrested
Dutch Revolutionary Socialist Worker's party (RSAP), forms
Australia snatches series against England 3-2 after being 2-0 down
American Bowling Congress' largest tournament (24,765 competitors)
American bandleader Artie Shaw records "Frenesi" on RCA Victor label
1st combat flight for Canadian British-built Avro Lancaster bomber
Battle of the Bismarck Sea: Australian and American air forces devastate Japanese navy convoy
Bethnal Green Tube disaster: 173 die in a stampede sheltering in an air raid, UK's greatest loss of civilian life in WWII (details censored till January 20 1945)
F Ryerson & Cohn Claues' "Harriet" premieres in NYC
RAF bombing error hits The Hague killing 511
Roermond & Venlo, Netherlands, liberated
US & Philippine forces recaptures Corregidor
US 7th Army occupies last part of Westwall
National-American Football League reverts to calling itself the NFL after 3 months
Bill Mikvy of Temple University scores NCAA basketball record 73 points, including 54 straight, in 93-69 win on the road at Wilkes College
Puerto Rico approves its 1st self-written constitution
Boston Braves, who own Milwaukee minor league franchise, block St Louis Browns attempt to shift their franchise to Milwaukee
Canadian Comet crashes at Karachi, 11 killed
Dutch swimmer Cockie Gastelaars breaks a 20-year-old world record for the 100m freestyle (1:04.2)
Indonesian government of Harahap resigns
Manhattan Borough President Hulan Jack makes plans for a new 110,000-seat stadium to entice Giants to stay in NY
Ice Dance Championship at Colorado Springs won by Markham & Jones of Great Britain
Ice Pairs Championship at Colorado Springs won by Wagner & Paul of Canada
Men's Figure Skating Champ in Colorado Springs won by David Jenkins of the USA
Worlds Ladies' Figure Skating Champ in Colorado Springs won by Carol Heiss (US)
KTVU TV channel 2 in Oakland-San Francisco, California (IND) 1st broadcast
Nuri ash Said becomes premier of Iraq
1st US probe to enter solar orbit, Pioneer 4, launched
British government arrests Hastings Banda of Nyasaland, ends emergency crisis
San Francisco Giants rename their stadium Candlestick Park
The Mesrop Mashtots Institute of Ancient Manuscripts opens in Yerevan, Armenia, with a collection of over 23,000 manuscripts
9th largest snowfall in NYC history (14.5")
British Antarctic Territory forms
American country blues musician "Mississippi" John Hurt 're-discovered' by musicologist Tom Hoskins in Avalon, Mississippi
Senegal adopts a new constitution
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Twister hits Jackson Mississippi; 3 minutes after 1st sighting, 57 die
WRFT (now WVFT) TV channel 27 in Roanoke, Virginia (IND) begins broadcasting
Grenada gains partial independence from Britain
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
White Sox given permission to use semi-DH in training camp with home club permission (use of pinch hitter twice in same game)
"Here's Where I Belong" opens/closes at Billy Rose Theater NYC
Greek, Portuguese & Spanish embassies bombed in the Hague
Pirate station "Radio Caroline" goes off the air when its ships Mi Amigo and Caroline are boarded and seized before the day's broadcasting began
Apollo 9 launched for 151 Earth orbits (10 days)
Turkish Airlines Flight 981 crashes in the Ermenonville Forest outside Paris, killing all 346 people onboard
5 workers are killed by the police in a demonstration in Vitoria-Gasteiz, Spain
Mozambique closes border with Rhodesia
Ice Pairs Champs at Tokyo won by Irina Rodnina/Alexandr Zaitsev (URS)
Islanders allow Red Wings only 11 shots on goal
Libyan Socialist Arabs People's Republic forms
Men's Figure Skating Champions in Tokyo won by Vladimir Kovalev (USSR)
1st day of Test cricket for Desmond Haynes (WI v Australia)
1st nuclear submarine, USS Nautilus is decommissioned
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Indian Mughal EmperorAkbardefeats Bengali army at the Battle of Tukaroi
Dutch privateerPiet Heynattacks and conquers 22 Portuguese ships in Bay of Salvador, Brazil
1st performance ofJoseph Haydn's 101st Symphony in D ("The Clock") at the Hanover Rooms, in London
US PresidentAndrew Jacksonand Congress recognize the Republic of Texas
1st performance ofFelix Mendelssohn's 3rd ("Scottish") Symphony in Leipzig Gewandhaus
Abraham Lincolnapproves charter for National Academy of Sciences
US Congress authorizes track width of 4'8½" forUnion Pacific Railroad
Rutherford B. Hayestakes the oath of office privately as official inauguration day falls on a Sunday
Anne Sullivan begins teaching 6-year-old blind-deafHelen Keller
George Deweybecomes 1st in US to hold the rank of Admiral of the Navy
EmperorWilhelm IIof Germany becomes the first person to make a sound recording of a political document, using Thomas Edison's cylinder
TsarNicholas IIof Russia agrees to summon a 'consultative assembly' and concedes other points including an edict of religious toleration, relief for Jews, and the cancellation of certain debts
John D. Rockefeller Jr. announces his retirement from managing his businesses so that he can be devoted full time to being a philanthropist at the Rockefeller Foundation
Elihu Rootfinishes his tenure as United States Senator from New York after choosing not to seek re-election
Cab Callowayrecords "Minnie the Moocher" (Jazz's 1st million seller)
John Dillingerbreaks out of jail using a wooden pistol
Clarrie Grimmettends his Test career with 13 wkts in 5th Test v South Africa
Mahatma Gandhibegins a fast in Mumbai (Bombay) to protest against autocratic rule in India
Netherlands NSB-leader Mussert visitsHermann Goeringin Berlin
Premiere performance of US Army CorporalSamuel Barber's 2nd Symphony by the Boston Symphony, conducted by Serge Koussevitzky in Boston, Massachusetts
Winston Churchillvisits Field Marshal Montgomery's headquarters
Elvis Presley's 1st hit in Billboard's top 10: "Heartbreak Hotel"
KingHassan IIascends to throne of Morocco
Canadian-American rock band "Buffalo Springfield" forms (Stephen Stills,Neil Young, et al)
Kwame Nkrumahflees Ghana to exile in Conakry, Guinea
In a blockbuster trade, Toronto Maple Leafs send future Hockey Hall of Fame left wingFrank Mahovlichto the rival Detroit Red Wings
Jean Béliveau(Montreal) becomes 2nd NHLer to score 1,000 pts
Apple Records releases Mary Hopkin's debut album "Post Card", in the US,  produced byPaul McCartney: US version substitutes her earlier hit singles for two tracks included on the UK version
South African Broadcasting Corp lifts its ban on the Beatles, begun afterJohn Lennon's infamous "Jesus" quote in 1966
Winnie Mandelasentenced to 1 year in jail in South Africa
Sculpted figures of Jefferson Davis,Robert E. LeeandStonewall Jacksonare completed at Stone Mountain Georgia
Linda McCartneyis charged in US with possession of marijuana
Pierre Trudeausworn in, for the second time, as the 17th Prime Minister of Canada
Alexander IIof Russia signs the Emancipation Manifesto, freeing serfs and granting them full rights as free citizens [O.S. Feb 19]
Georges Bizet's last and greatest opera "Carmen" premieres at the Opéra-Comique in Paris, France

Woman suffrage procession through Washington, D.C. organized byAlice Pauland Lucy Burns and led by Inez Milholland. Ida B. Wells marched with her Illinois delegation despite blacks being told to march in a separate section.
